catalog.title: Listen & read · Who said what? Listen, read, respond
catalog.description: Who said what? Listen, read, respond · A2 listen & read in “Food & shopping”: Dutch you can use the same week, guided steps, and a short check at the end.

pedagogy.objective: By the end of this lesson, you can understand short input about food & shopping, notice useful chunks, and answer gist/detail questions
can_do_outcomes: Ask for items and quantities in a supermarket, market, or bakery
Say what you like or avoid (allergies, vegetarian) in simple sentences
Follow a short exchange in this theme and reuse one chunk.
pedagogy.grammar_primary_label: Modals for requests: willen, kunnen, mogen

grammar_points.point: modal verbs: willen, kunnen, mogen (polite requests)
examples_en: May I have a receipt, please?

grammar_points.point: measure words and containers (een kilo, een pak, een fles)
examples_nl: Neemt u een mandje bij de ingang.
examples_en: Take a basket at the entrance.

examples_nl: Deze kaas is iets goedkoper vandaag.
Ik vind vers brood lekkerder dan uit de diepvries.
examples_en: This cheese is a bit cheaper today.
I prefer fresh bread to frozen.

micro_outcomes: I can recognise key words about food & shopping in short Dutch texts or audio.
I can copy at least one useful chunk into a sentence about myself.
I can finish most guided tasks in the lesson without peeking at translations on the second try.
I can pass the quick self-check at the end of the lesson.
I know what to revise tomorrow in two minutes or less.

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Understand short input about food & shopping, notice useful chunks, and answer gist/detail questions

**Grammar thread today:** Modals for requests: willen, kunnen, mogen — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Food & shopping**.

Words you might use today:
• **boodschappen**
• **bonuskaart**
• **kassa**
• **bon**
• **pinnen**
• **contant**
• **vegetarisch**
• **notenallergie**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.example_response: Gisteren ging ik naar de supermarkt en kocht ik brood en groente. 's Middags maakte ik een salade en 's avonds at ik met een vriend in een klein restaurant.

steps.activity:
**Listen & read** this short conversation. Read twice: first for gist, then for details.

**Klant:** Goedemiddag. Waar vind ik de lactosevrije yoghurt?

**Medewerker:** Gangpad 5, rechts. Er is ook plantaardige kaas bij de koeling.

**Klant:** Dank u. En heeft u deze week aanbieding op appels?

**Medewerker:** Ja, een kilo voor twee euro, bij de groente.

**Klant:** Perfect. Dan neem ik ook een mandje.

**Gist check:** Who are the people, and what is the situation?
**Detail check:** Note one time phrase or place you recognise.

---

**Script — Albert Heijn (checkout)**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| Mag ik een bon, alstublieft? | May I have a receipt, please? |
| Ik pin graag contactloos. | I'd like to pay contactless. |
| Statiegeld flesjes, alstublieft. | Bottle deposit refund, please. |

**Listen task:** play TTS on the Dutch column once, then cover the English and say what each line means.

**Today's grammar — modals (requests & permission)**

**Rule:** *Willen* (want), *kunnen* (can), *mogen* (may) + infinitive at the end: *Ik **wil** graag **betalen***.

**Patterns:**
• *Mag ik een bon?* · *Kunt u me helpen?* · *Ik kan vandaag niet komen.*

**Produce:**
1. Ask permission with *Mag ik …?* for something you need in a shop.
2. Polite *Kunt u …* vs informal *Kun je …* — write one of each.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — ui / eu / ij:** round lips for *huis — leeuw*; *mijn* is one smooth glide.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**modal verbs: willen, kunnen, mogen (polite requests)**
• NL: Ik wil graag halfvolle melk. / Mag ik een bon, alstublieft?
• EN: I'd like semi-skimmed milk. / May I have a receipt, please?

**measure words and containers (een kilo, een pak, een fles)**
• NL: Neemt u een mandje bij de ingang. / Kiest u pin of contant?
• EN: Take a basket at the entrance. / Choose card or cash?

**comparatives (goedkoper, lekkerder) in short opinions**
• NL: Deze kaas is iets goedkoper vandaag. / Ik vind vers brood lekkerder dan uit de diepvries.
• EN: This cheese is a bit cheaper today. / I prefer fresh bread to frozen.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.


**Culture tip:** Supermarkets like Albert Heijn and Jumbo are widespread; many people bring their own bags (statiegeld bottles returned to machines).
*How does this compare with where you live? One sentence in your own words.*

**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)
• **boodschappen** ↔ a word you connect with daily life
• **bonuskaart** ↔ a place or thing from this unit

**2 — Gap-fill**
Complete: Ik _____ om acht uur. *(opstaan → stem: Ik **sta** … **op**)*

**3 — Mini dialogue**
**A:** Hoe laat ben je klaar? **B:** Om half zes, denk ik.

**4 — Find the odd one**
Which word fits *least* with this unit: **kassa**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

**5 — True / false**
Say **waar** or **niet waar**: "In Dutch, the verb often moves when you ask a yes/no question."

**6 — Your sentence**
Write one true sentence about yourself using **boodschappen** or **bonuskaart**.

**7 — Transform**
Make this informal line a bit more formal: "Kun je me helpen?" → start with **Kunt u …**

**8 — Quick translation check**
"Ik wil graag halfvolle melk." — what does it mean in English (your own words)?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Klant: Goedemiddag. Waar vind ik de lactosevrije yoghurt?*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Klant: Goedemiddag. Waar vind ik de lactosevrije yoghurt?*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **boodschappen** and fits *Modals for requests: willen, kunnen, mogen*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

steps.activity:
**Phrase bank (use freely)**
• boodschappen
• bonuskaart
• kassa
• bon
• pinnen
• contant
• vegetarisch
• notenallergie
• aanbieding
• vers
• diepvries
• mandje

**Your task:** Write or say **4–6 short sentences** in Dutch about this unit theme.
Rules: include **at least one question** and one connector (**en**, **maar**, or **want**).

**Example starter (you can change it):**
Vandaag … Ik wil … **want** … **En jij?**

**Quick self-check**

Try each question, then tap **Check answer**. The correct answer stays hidden until you submit.

**Tip:** Say Dutch options out loud if that helps.

**Afterwards:** If three or more felt easy, open **Quiz** for a short recap. If not, go back to **Guided practice** once more.
steps.teacher_notes: Rendered with interactive UI when interaction is present.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **boodschappen**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag boodschappen met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next in this unit: **Listen & read · Mini-dialogues from everyday life** — we keep building **Food & shopping** with a new focus.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

**Albert Heijn**, **Jumbo**, **Lidl**, and **Dirk** are everyday names; many people shop several times a week for fresh bread and veg. Bringing your own bag is normal; bottle returns (*statiegeld*) at the machine are part of the routine.

**Phrases — supermarket & paying**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Mag ik een bon, alstublieft?* | May I have a receipt, please? |
| *Ik pin graag contactloos.* | I'd like to pay contactless. |
| *Waar vind ik de lactosevrije melk?* | Where do I find lactose-free milk? |

**Mini dialogue**

**Klant:** Goedemiddag, ik zoek volkoren brood en kaas zonder noten.
**Medewerker:** De kaas is bij de toonbank; het brood ligt in gangpad twee.
**Klant:** Dank u. Ik betaal bij zelfscankassa vier.

**Say or write:** Ask where one product is using *Waar vind ik …?*

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?
steps.teacher_notes: Link to next lesson id in CMS when wired.

quiz_ideas: Self-check: choose the best reply to "Hoe gaat het?" (see step 7 in the lesson).
Gap-fill idea: write a sentence with **boodschappen** in the present tense.
True/false: in Dutch yes/no questions the verb often comes before the subject.
Say one new polite request starting with **Kunt u …** or **Mag ik …**.
Paraphrase one Dutch line from the lesson input in your own English.
assessment.success_criteria: You can complete the step-7 self-check comfortably and produce at least one clear Dutch sentence about the lesson theme (spoken or written).

content_refs.notes: Slow audio assumed for lines marked *slow* in builder CMS.
